enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses themes of love and admiration, showcasing the beauty of romance through its lyrics.

In which movie does this song appear?

Andha Arabic Kadaloram is featured in the movie Bombay, which explores social issues and love.

Who are the singers of this song?

The song is sung by AR Rahman and Suresh Peters, both of whom bring their unique styles to the track.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Andha Arabic Kadaloram was composed by the renowned AR Rahman.

When was this song released?

The song was released on March 10, 1995.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the movie Bombay, Andha Arabic Kadaloram serves as a vibrant expression of love and longing. The song's upbeat tempo and romantic lyrics evoke feelings of joy and celebration, making it perfect for weddings and special occasions. It beautifully captures the essence of youthful love amidst the backdrop of societal challenges. With its catchy melody and heartfelt delivery, this track remains a beloved classic in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Andha Arabic Kadaloram from the Bombay soundtrack is a mesmerizing blend of AR Rahman's enchanting music and the soulful voices of AR Rahman and Suresh Peters. This song captures the essence of romance and joy, making it a timeless classic. Experience the vibrant energy and lyrical beauty that has made this track a favorite among fans.
stanzaMeanings: I saw that beautiful Arabic sea, I saw those eyes like a gentle breeze.
I saw your lotus feet, I saw your beautiful song in the right way.
I saw the path coming, it was so pleasant.
The tender fingers touched my heart, oh what a feeling it is..
The heart is fluttering with excitement, a hundred kisses in different ways.
With fear and shyness, I am overwhelmed, oh what a divine feeling it is.
I saw that beautiful Arabic sea, I saw those eyes like a gentle breeze.
I saw your lotus feet, I saw your beautiful song in the right way, oh yes, oh yes.
After giving a kiss, there is a kiss on the lips.
Even after the lamp is extinguished, there is light in the eyes.
Even after the sky has fallen, there is joy on the earth.
Even after desire has faded, love still remains.
I saw that beautiful Arabic sea, I saw those eyes like a gentle breeze.
